parts.text: Complete the table to show the business document and book of prime entry for each transaction.
parts.answer: Transaction 2: Credit note, Sales returns journal. Transaction 3: Paying-in slip, Cash book. Transaction 4: Bank statement, Cash book.

parts.text: Prepare Petra's account for the month of August 2020.
parts.answer: Aug 1: Balance b/d $940. Aug 14: Sales returns $120. Aug 9: Sales $675. Aug 29: Bank $893. Discount allowed $47. Aug 31: Balance c/d $555. Total $1,615. Sept 1: Balance b/d $555.

parts.text: State the name of the item shown in Petra's account as a current asset.
parts.answer: Current assets

parts.text: Advise Dev whether to offer a cash discount to customers.
parts.answer: Advantages: Increase profit for the year, Increase cash inflow. Disadvantages: May lose customers/sales may fall, Customers may take longer to pay.

parts.text: Advise Dev whether to convert to a limited company.
parts.answer: Advantages: Dev will have limited liability for the debts of the company, The limited company will have a separate legal identity to Dev, Dev will have access to different forms of finance/increase in capital employed. Disadvantages: The limited company will have greater regulation than Dev as a sole trader, The accounting requirements of the limited company will be more complex than for Dev as a sole trader, The financial statements of the limited company may be viewed by the public unlike those of Dev as a sole trader. Recommendation required.

parts.text: Prepare the subscriptions account for the year ended 31 July 2020.
parts.answer: Aug 1: Balance b/d $360. Jul 31: Bank $9,960. Balance c/d $720. Jul 31: Income and Expenditure $10,200. Balance c/d $120. Total $11,040. Aug 1: Balance b/d $720, Balance b/d $120.

parts.text: Prepare the trading account for the café for the year ended 31 July 2020.
parts.answer: Revenue $13,520. Cost of sales: Opening inventory $520, Purchases $8,190 = $8,710 - Closing inventory $770 = $7,940. Wages - café staff $2,770. Profit for the year $2,810.

parts.text: Prepare the income and expenditure account for the year ended 31 July 2020.
parts.answer: Income: Subscriptions $10,200, Profit on café $2,810 = $13,010. Expenditure: Wages - sports staff $5,220, Insurance - sports hall and sports equipment $1,390, Sports Hall expenses $3,360, Depreciation - sports hall $440, Depreciation - sports equipment $600 = $11,010. Surplus for the year $2,000.

parts.text: Define the term 'accumulated fund'.
parts.answer: The difference between the assets and the liabilities. The accumulated surpluses earned since the club started.

parts.text: Calculate the accumulated fund at 1 August 2019.
parts.answer: Increase $2,000. Accumulated fund at 1 August 2019 = Accumulated fund at 31 July 2020 - Increase = $2,000.

parts.text: Calculate the cost of sales for the year ended 30 September 2020.
parts.answer: Opening inventory $66,000 + Purchases $263,000 + Carriage inwards $1,500 = $330,500 - Closing inventory $59,000 = $271,500.

parts.text: Calculate the increase in the provision for doubtful debts.
parts.answer: Provision at 30 September 2020 ((80,500 - 500) x 2%) = $1,600. Less Provision at 1 October 2019 $1,000. Increase in provision for doubtful debts $600.

parts.text: Prepare the income statement for the year ended 30 September 2020.
parts.answer: Revenue $529,500. Less Cost of sales $271,500 = Gross profit $258,000. Less expenses: Administrative expenses (44,000 - 1,200) $42,800, Distribution costs (38,000 + 800) $38,800, Irrecoverable debts $500, Provision for doubtful debts $600, Depreciation - non-current assets $61,000 = $143,700. Profit from operations $114,300. Debenture interest (700 + 700) $1,400. Profit for the year $112,900.

parts.text: State the effect of each item on capital employed.
parts.answer: Issue of additional debentures: Increase. Issue of additional ordinary shares: Increase. Payment of ordinary share dividend: No effect. Proposal of ordinary share dividend: No effect. Transfer from retained earnings to general reserve: No effect.

parts.text: Calculate the profit for the year ended 30 September 2020.
Calculate the profit margin, gross margin, and return on capital employed.
Comment on the performance of JP Limited.
parts.answer: Profit margin: Has worsened from 8.56% to 6.67%. Possible causes: Increase in expenses, Poor control over expenses. Gross margin: Has worsened from 34.26% to 29.58%. Possible causes: Reduction in selling price, Purchasing from more expensive suppliers/increased cost of sales. Return on capital employed: Has worsened from 32.08% to 25.81%. Possible causes: Increased capital employed, Less efficient use of its resources.

parts.text: Advise JP Limited whether to introduce additional capital or take a loan.
parts.answer: Introduce additional capital: Advantages - Does not have to be repaid, No interest cost. Disadvantages - May not have enough available funds, Greater personal risk. Loan: Advantages - Instantly available, Has two years to pay it off. Disadvantages - Annual interest is charged, Must be repaid, Security may be required. Recommendation required.

parts.text: Identify interested parties and their interest.
parts.answer: Suppliers/potential suppliers: To assess whether outstanding debts are likely to be paid. Bank: To assess the likelihood of loan/overdraft being repaid when due, To assess the ability to pay interest on a loan/overdraft, To assess the availability of security for a loan. Lenders/potential lenders: To assess the likelihood of a loan being repaid when due, To assess the ability to pay interest on a loan, To assess the availability of security for a loan. Investors/potential partners: To assess future prospects of the business, To assess profitability. Government/tax authorities: To assess the tax due from the owner of the business.

parts.text: Prepare the suspense account.
parts.answer: Sep 30: Sales returns $300, Difference on trial balance $200, Motor repairs $180, Rent receivable $310, Kamil $900, Rent payable $310. Total $1,100.

parts.text: Identify the type of error for errors 2 and 4.
parts.answer: Error 2: Error of omission. Error 4: Error of transposition in one account.

parts.text: State the effect of each error on profit.
parts.answer: Error 1: Increase profit. Error 2: Decrease profit. Error 3: No effect on profit. Error 4: Decrease profit. Error 5: Increase profit. Error 6: Decrease profit. Error 7: Increase profit.

parts.text: Advise Razia whether to sell only for cash.
parts.answer: Advantages: Less book-keeping, No irrecoverable debts, Cash received immediately. Disadvantages: May lose customers/sales may reduce, May adversely affect customer relationships/customer loyalty, Increased security issues. Recommendation required.
